question: Which of the following is true for an aircraft with positive static stability?

options.text: An aircraft that has positive static stability sees the oscillations continue to either side of the original attitude but become smaller when it is disturbed.
An aircraft that has positive static stability tends to return to its original attitude when it is disturbed.
An aircraft that has positive static stability initially tends to return to its original attitude when it is disturbed and continues in that direction.
An aircraft that has positive static stability remains in its attitude when it is disturbed.
